research_summary: East Auckland suburban market in April 2026 shows diverging micro-trends. Botany Downs and Flat Bush corridor continues to attract first-home buyers and young families due to relative affordability vs inner-city Auckland, strong schooling zones (Botany Downs Secondary, Sancta Maria College), and the Eastern Busway extension providing improved public transport links. Flat Bush median sits around $1.24M with the highest transaction volume in the area at ~623 sales/year, signalling sustained demand. The OCR at 2.25% and mid-4% fixed mortgage rates have improved serviceability. New townhouse and terrace developments in Flat Bush under the Medium Density Residential Standards are adding supply, but demand from growing South and East Asian communities keeps the area resilient. Botany town centre retail and employment hub acts as a lifestyle anchor reinforcing liveability scores.
post_text:
Flat Bush is quietly becoming one of East Auckland's most in-demand suburbs — and it's not hard to see why. 🏡

Great schools, easy access to Botany town centre, and a median price around $1.24M that still feels achievable compared to the rest of Auckland. With the Eastern Busway improving commute times and new townhouse developments adding options for all budgets, this suburb is ticking a lot of boxes.

If you're exploring East Auckland and haven't looked at Flat Bush yet — it might just surprise you. 👇
call_to_action: Curious what your budget gets you in Flat Bush right now? DM me and let's take a look together.
